rulings.Case Title: THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. FINANCIAL CASUALTY & SURETY, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment; Discharge Forfeiture And Exonerate Bail, Etc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2025, Line 1, DEFENDANT FINANCIAL CASUALTY & SURETY, INC.'s Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment; Discharge Forfeiture And Exonerate Bail, Etc. Defendant Financial Casualty & Surety, Inc.'s Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment; Discharge Forfeiture And Exonerate Bai is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Where, as here, bail is ordered forfeited, the clerk must timely mail notice of the forfeiture to the surety and the bail agent, as well as execute a certificate of mailing documenting their actions. (Pen. Code section 1305(b).) Defendant has submitted credible evidence that it never received notice of forfeiture. (See Ramos Decl.) The City and County of San Francisco filed an untimely opposition, but even there, the City did not produce evidence that a timely mailing occurred or a certificate of mailing was executed. It follows Defendant was without notice of the summary judgment proceedings that led to the judgment. The judgment, thus, is vacated. (See CCP section 473.) The time for Defendant to respond to the forfeiture by, for example, moving to set aside forfeiture of the bond and to exonerate bail, is extended. The statutory time shall commence from date the court files this order. The motion is denied to the extent Defendant seeks different or additional relief. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: GREGORY KRIKORIAN VS. MICHAEL SCHINNER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2025, Line 3, DEFENDANTS MICHAEL SCHINNER, AND SCHINNER & SHAIN, LLP'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. Off calendar. On February 15, 2024, this court issued an order staying this action. The order provides that "[t]his case is therefore stayed pending further order of the court." (Order re Defendants Schinner and Schinner & Shain LLP's Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings, 2:9-10.) There is nothing in the record showing that this court issued a subsequent order lifting the stay. The improperly noticed motion for judgment on the pleadings is therefore off calendar. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.)
